title: Tangy Greek Revithia Jjigae
description: A Korean twist on traditional Greek revithia, with a tangy and spicy kick. This dish is served in a molten, one-pot wonder that's perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Korean brass bowl over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the cumin and dried oregano and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the grand marnier and cook until the liquid is almost completely evaporated.
Add the gochujang and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Stir in the revithia, black pepper, and salt, and cook until the liquid has thickened and the revithia are coated.
Transfer the contents of the bowl to a serving platter or individual plates.
Top with crumbly feta cheese, fresh parsley, and a drizzle of sesame oil.
Serve immediately, garnished with ssam wrap.
Garnish with additional red pepper flakes if desired.
Serve with a side of kimchi or other spicy condiment, if desired.
Garnish with additional parsley if desired.
